answers.answer: C) "The question isn't who made the error - it's why our system allowed a single config change to cause an outage. If one missed checklist step can take down production, we need guardrails: automated config validation, canary deployments, or mandatory peer review for config changes". Reason: This is the essence of blameless post-mortems. Focus on SYSTEMIC issues, not individual blame. The real problem: (1) No automated validation; (2) No canary/safe rollout; (3) No peer review requirement. Option A blames the junior. Option B avoids the issue. Option D adds process without fixing root cause.

SLACK DM TO DANA (Sunday evening):

---
Hey Dana! 👋 Welcome back! Hope you had a restful few months.

Just wanted to give you a quick heads-up before Monday: no rush to dive in headfirst. Let's plan a gentle ramp-up - maybe start with catching up on project docs and joining standups, and we can schedule a 1:1 Monday afternoon to go over anything you want to prioritize.

The team's excited to have you back! If you need anything - flexible hours, lighter load, whatever - just let me know. We'll figure it out together.

See you Monday! 🎉
---

TEAM MESSAGE IN #ENGINEERING:

---
@channel Hey team! Dana is back starting Monday - welcome back! 🎉

Dana, we've got some updates to catch you up on, but no rush. Let's give you space to settle back in. Everyone, please say hi and feel free to share any relevant context Dana might need as they ramp up.

Looking forward to having the full team back together! 👋
---

Key points:
- DM: Warm, sets expectations (gentle ramp-up), offers flexibility, makes them feel valued
- Team msg: Welcoming, no mention of medical leave, guides team on how to help (share context)
